name: Newcode.ai

facts.founded: unknown (sources conflict: Crunchbase/EU-Startups list 2021 and 2024; Helmy interview implies ~2022-2023 — treat as unconfirmed)
facts.lead_investor: Alliance VC and The Legal Tech Fund (with participation from law firms); no single lead clearly named
integrations: Positioned as an intelligence layer across a firm's systems, data and applications — specific named connectors (iManage/Word/etc.) not confirmed in credible sources
facts.agentic_interop: Explicitly agentic: markets 'agentic AI workflows' and an 'AI-native operating system for legal' where firms delegate complex multi-step work and receive complete deliverables. Auditability/control/deployment-flexibility emphasized. No specific API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent protocol signals confirmed in public sources.
facts.tech_model: Agentic, model-agnostic generative AI platform acting as an intelligence/orchestration layer over firm data; founder background in deep learning / enterprise AI for regulated environments.
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Newcode.ai, Oslo-based legal AI founded by Maged Helmy (PhD, Univ. of Oslo), $6.5M raise, DLA Piper partner. Ruled out unrelated 'newcode' software/dev-tool naming and UbiOps (separate Dutch MLOps firm — no documented link).

uniqueness.justification: Agentic 'OS for legal' framing is differentiated and Nordics-anchored, but the 'AI operating system' positioning is now crowded; thin proof of unique tech.

thesis_fit.justification: Strong fit on 'AI as preparation, accountability, supervised autonomy' — agentic, deliverable-oriented, stresses auditability/control; loses a point as interop depth is unproven.

investability.justification: Real $6.5M seed, credible Nordic VCs and a marquee anchor client (DLA Piper), but early/small and single big-customer concentration.

defensibility.justification: Conservative: moat is embedded-team workflow design + Nordic firm relationships; no proprietary-data or protocol lock-in evidenced.

interoperability.justification: Claims to live across firm systems and run agentic multi-step workflows, but no confirmed API/MCP/CLI/named-DMS integrations — claim outruns evidence.

team.justification: Founder Maged Helmy has a deep-learning PhD and 8+ yrs building enterprise AI for regulated environments; associate professor — strong technical pedigree.

provisional_verdict: Technically-real agentic player with a marquee Nordic anchor (DLA Piper) but early and integration depth unproven. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Founding year inconsistent across sources
Headcount and pricing unknown
Heavy reliance on one flagship customer (DLA Piper) and self/PR-reported capability claims
No confirmed named integrations or open protocols (API/MCP/CLI)

sources.what_it_supports: $6.5M seed, founder Maged Helmy, agentic OS-for-legal positioning
Funding, AI operating system framing, Norway HQ
DLA Piper strategic partnership, agentic workflows across Nordics, embedded team
Norway startup, $6.5M raise
Company profile / founding-year data (conflicting)

name: LUPL

facts.founded: Spun out of 'Project Aries' ~2019-2020; publicly launched 2020-2021
facts.hq: Reston, Virginia, USA (also Singapore roots; founding firms incl. Singapore's Rajah & Tann)
facts.lead_investor: Founding/strategic investors: CMS, Cooley, Rajah & Tann Asia (law firms)
facts.headcount: unknown (early reports referenced ~50 developers building it ~2020; current headcount unconfirmed)
customers: Used by law firms and in-house legal departments (founding firms CMS, Cooley, Rajah & Tann); specific named paying customers not separately confirmed
integrations: Open API for Workstreams (create Workstreams/items via API)
facts.agentic_interop: Open API (Workstreams endpoints) confirmed; a 'Lupl for Copilot' agent inside Teams; native DMS connectors (iManage/NetDocuments) and MS 365 surface. Strong on 'lives where lawyers work' interop; lighter on autonomous-agent / MCP signals.
facts.tech_model: Matter-synchronization / legal project & task management platform ('open industry platform for legal matters') unifying people, conversations, documents and data across firms and clients; integrates with DMS and MS 365 rather than being a generative-AI engine itself.
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Lupl, legal matter/project-management platform (lupl.com), founded out of CMS/Cooley/Rajah & Tann collaboration; co-founder/CPO Matt Pollins (ex-CMS partner). No significant naming collision found.

uniqueness.justification: 'Open industry platform / universal adapter for legal matters' is a distinctive cross-firm collaboration concept, but practice/matter-management is a crowded category.

thesis_fit.justification: Strong on 'lives where lawyers work' (DMS + MS 365 + API) but it's primarily PM/collaboration, not agentic supervised autonomy; AI is a thin Copilot layer.

investability.justification: Real $24M and blue-chip law-firm backers, but last confirmed raise was Jan 2021 — no recent round evidenced, raising staleness/runway questions.

defensibility.justification: Moat from deep DMS/MS 365 integrations + multi-firm consortium relationships; offset by commoditized PM category.

interoperability.justification: Genuinely interoperable: open API, native iManage/NetDocuments, Teams/Outlook/Copilot — clearly engineered to live inside lawyers' tools.

team.justification: Credible founding team (ex-CMS partner Matt Pollins) and law-firm sponsorship; full leadership/scale details limited in public sources.

provisional_verdict: Well-integrated legal matter/PM platform that lives where lawyers work, but funding looks stale and AI is a thin Copilot layer rather than agentic. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: No funding round confirmed since Jan 2021 — possible stagnation
Current headcount unknown
AI/agentic capability is shallow (Copilot wrapper) relative to thesis
Pricing unknown

sources.what_it_supports: Company description, co-founder Matt Pollins, Project Aries origin
$14M funding round
$14M Jan 2021, CMS/Cooley backing
$24M total raised, HQ Reston VA, investors
Integrations: iManage, NetDocuments, Teams, Outlook, Copilot, open API

name: August

facts.hq: unknown (US-focused; august.law/us — specific city not confirmed)
customers: Midsize and boutique law firms and in-house legal teams (specific named customers not confirmed); early adopters cited generically
facts.agentic_interop: Agent-based architecture with modular/configurable AI 'agents' and custom workflows; integrates across web, Word and Outlook with context preservation; DMS connectors (iManage, NetDocs, SharePoint, GDrive, Dropbox). 'Lives where lawyers work.' No explicit MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent protocol confirmed.
facts.tech_model: Configurable legal-AI workspace: modular agents/workflows adapt to a firm's playbooks, statutes and output formats for research, review, drafting and due diligence; self-serve + bespoke onboarding led by attorneys and engineers.
facts.pricing: Self-serve with two-week free trial; paid tiers not publicly itemized (treat specific pricing as unknown)
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = August, legal AI workspace at august.law, $7M seed led by NEA + Pear VC, founders Rutvik Rao, Thomas Bueler-Faudree, Joseph Parker (met at Columbia). Ruled out the very common 'August' name in other domains (e.g., August smart locks/home, August media, etc.) — this is the legal-AI 'August'.

uniqueness.justification: Explicit focus on the underserved midsize/boutique segment with configurable, playbook-adaptive agents is a sharp positioning, though drafting/review is a crowded space.

thesis_fit.justification: Good fit: agent-based, configurable workflows, deep DMS/Word/Outlook integration — preparation + supervised autonomy where lawyers work.

investability.justification: $7M seed co-led by NEA and Pear VC with strong angels (Gokul Rajaram, Ramp CPO, OpenAI eng lead) signals high-quality conviction at seed.

defensibility.justification: Conservative: moat rests on configurability + onboarding + segment focus; thin firm-specific data lock-in evidenced; competes with Harvey/Spellbook/CoCounsel etc.

interoperability.justification: Confirmed integrations into Word, Outlook, SharePoint, iManage, NetDocuments, Google Drive, Dropbox with context preservation — genuinely embedded.

team.justification: Technical founders (Columbia CS/ML lab) with experience at Blackstone data science, DoorDash, PayPal; strong investor/angel network.

provisional_verdict: Technically-real, well-integrated configurable drafting/review agent for the underserved midsize segment, strongly backed at seed. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: No named customers confirmed (only generic 'early adopters')
HQ city and headcount unknown
Defensibility thin in a crowded drafting/review market
Performance/time-savings stats are self-reported

sources.what_it_supports: $7M seed, NEA + Pear VC, founders, founded 2023, midsize focus
Lead investor rationale, configurable modular agents
Self-serve launch, integrations (SharePoint/iManage/NetDocs/GDrive/Dropbox), Word/Outlook, agent-based architecture
Self-serve platform, free trial, drafting/due-diligence capabilities
Product positioning: research/review/draft, configurable workflows

name: Aloi

facts.last_round: unknown (seed-stage; exact round structure/date not confirmed in credible primary sources)
facts.lead_investor: Dagg AI listed as an investor (per aggregator); lead investor not clearly confirmed
customers: Law firms / legal teams (positioned for firms where confidentiality and control are non-negotiable); specific named customers not confirmed
integrations: Deploys within the firm's own environment/infrastructure; permission-aware; specific named connectors (iManage/Word/etc.) not confirmed
facts.agentic_interop: Markets an 'autonomous legal ecosystem' with audit trails and in-environment deployment; precedent discovery, drafting and negotiation support. No confirmed API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent or named-integration signals — interop claims are high-level.
facts.tech_model: Institutional-knowledge intelligence layer: ingests a firm's contracts, redlines, approvals and negotiation history to power precedent discovery, long-form drafting and grounded counterproposals; secure, in-firm-environment, permission-aware deployment.
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Aloi (aloi.law), Stockholm legal-knowledge/contract-intelligence startup founded 2024. Ruled out: Aloi Global / Aloi Technologies (Singapore/Nepal fintech for grassroots loan financing, founded 2019) and individual attorney 'Paul M. Aloi' — both unrelated.

uniqueness.justification: Institutional-memory-to-decision-grade-intelligence framing (precedent + negotiation history) is a reasonable angle, but knowledge-management AI is increasingly contested.

thesis_fit.justification: Good thesis alignment: preparation (precedent/knowledge), accountability (audit trails, permission-aware, in-firm deployment), supervised drafting/negotiation.

investability.justification: Conservative: ~$6.29M is aggregator-only with no confirmed press round; lead investor unclear; early and lightly documented.

defensibility.justification: Thin evidence: claimed moat is a firm's own institutional data, but no proprietary tech, protocol or lock-in confirmed; mark low.

interoperability.justification: Only high-level 'deploy in your environment, permission-aware' claims; no confirmed API/MCP/CLI or named integrations — interop unproven.

team.justification: Self-described 'founded by lawyers, built by AI/infra/enterprise experts' but no founder names or verifiable track record found in credible sources.

provisional_verdict: Plausible firm-knowledge/contract-intelligence play with the right thesis framing, but thinly evidenced — founders, round and integrations unconfirmed. [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: Founder names and team track record not confirmed
Funding ($6.29M) and investor only from aggregators, not primary/press
No confirmed integrations or open protocols
Headcount and pricing unverified
Name collides with unrelated fintech 'Aloi Global'

sources.what_it_supports: Positioning: institutional knowledge to decision-grade intelligence, founded-by-lawyers claim, in-firm deployment
Precedent discovery, drafting, negotiation support, audit trail
Founded 2024, Stockholm HQ, funding figure
$6.29M funding, ~20 employees, Dagg AI investor
Name-collision ruled out: unrelated fintech Aloi Global (Singapore/Nepal)

name: PhaseLaw

facts.lead_investor: Pear VC (round led by Pear, with Defined, BoxGroup, Twelve Below)
customers: Teams handling Subject Access Requests (DSARs), regulatory inquiries and litigation disclosure; enterprises needing large-scale redaction. Specific named customers not confirmed.
integrations: Cloud-based, single-tenant, region-selectable hosting; SOC2 Type II. No specific named DMS/ediscovery-platform connectors or open API/MCP confirmed.
facts.agentic_interop: AI-native, human-in-the-loop document review/redaction workflow ('Redact Once, Apply Everywhere') across emails, chats, docs, images; AI models recognize 50+ categories of personal data. Workflow automation is strong, but no confirmed API/MCP/CLI/agent-to-agent interop signals.
facts.tech_model: AI-powered, human-in-the-loop eDiscovery & redaction platform: deduplication, threading, relevance review and consistent rules-based redaction in one workflow; single-tenant cloud, built by petabyte-scale data/AI engineers.
facts.name_collision_check: Confirmed entity = Phaselaw by Phaselab Inc. (phase.law), NYC eDiscovery/redaction startup, founded 2023 by Josh Schwartz (ex-CTO Chartbeat, ex-MIT CSAIL) and David van Dokkum, $2.8M seed led by Pear (Apr 2025). Ruled out generic 'phase'/'phase law' usage and physics 'phase' terms.

uniqueness.justification: Sharp wedge: AI-native DSAR/redaction with 'redact once, apply everywhere' and 50+ PII categories is a focused, differentiated take on eDiscovery.

thesis_fit.justification: Fits 'AI as preparation + accountability' (human-in-the-loop, compliance) but it's a workflow tool, not broadly agentic/supervised-autonomy; bounded scope.

investability.justification: Real $2.8M seed from credible early-stage funds (Pear, BoxGroup), but small/early with no follow-on evidenced.

defensibility.justification: Conservative: PII-detection models + UX are the edge, but eDiscovery/redaction is competitive (Relativity, Reveal, etc.); no clear data/protocol moat.

interoperability.justification: Single-tenant cloud with region choice and SOC2, but no confirmed named integrations or open API/MCP — appears more standalone than embedded.

team.justification: Strong technical founders: Josh Schwartz (ex-CTO Chartbeat, MIT CSAIL) + David van Dokkum; team cites 30+ yrs petabyte-scale data/AI experience.

provisional_verdict: Focused, well-built AI-native redaction/DSAR tool with a strong technical team, but narrow scope and standalone (low interop). [PROVISIONAL]
red_flags: No named customers confirmed
Headcount and pricing unknown
Appears standalone — no confirmed DMS/ediscovery integrations or open API/MCP
Narrow product scope (redaction/DSAR) limits TAM vs thesis

sources.what_it_supports: Product intro, eDiscovery/redaction positioning, fundraising
$2.8M raise, April 2025 timing
Seed round April 21 2025, $2.8M, Pear/Defined/BoxGroup/Twelve Below
Founders Josh Schwartz & David van Dokkum, founded 2023, NYC, human-in-the-loop, 50+ PII categories, SOC2
Josh Schwartz background: CEO Phaselaw, ex-CTO Chartbeat, ex-MIT CSAIL
